task: If Eliud Kipchoge could maintain his record-making marathon pace indefinitely, how many thousand hours would it take him to run the distance between the Earth and the Moon its closest approach? Please use the minimum perigee value on the Wikipedia page for the Moon when carrying out your calculation. Round your result to the nearest 1000 hours and do not use any comma separators if necessary.
graderReasoning: The agent successfully completed the task by first identifying the minimum perigee distance of the Moon from Earth on Wikipedia, which was 356,400 km. It then correctly used Eliud Kipchoge's marathon world record time (2 hours, 1 minute, 9 seconds for 42.195 km) to calculate his average pace. Finally, it calculated the total time required to cover the perigee distance at that pace, converted it to hours, and rounded the result to the nearest thousand hours as requested. The final answer of 17 thousand hours matches the ground truth.
